Later, he moved to Hollywood, drove a cab and worked as a waiter. His father told him to stop the "craziness" and get a job, as he had a wife and daughter. After his brother's murder in 1983, he moved back and started writing, leading to "Half-Deserted Streets" about his brother's death. The play led to screenplay work. He faced more struggles until 2005 when "Crash" won awards, making him a success at 54. Now, let's check each option: Option A: "His father did not support his work as a bartender." The option says "bartender," which isn't mentioned. So A might be the incorrect statement. Option B: "Before he became an overnight success, his life experienced ups and downs." Option C: "His brother's death inspired his writing Half-Deserted Streets." The passage states he started writing as a way to explore the pain of Hell's Kitchen after his brother's death in 1983, and Half-Deserted Streets was based on his brother's killing. So C is true.
